proposed_from.topic: Sharp partial identification of continuous-treatment dose-response curves under monotone-and-concave shape restrictions and bounded unmeasured confounding, without an instrument
proposed_from.novelty_justification: {"repo_axis":"The closest in-catalogue ThmSmith proposals remain q1_partial_did_unobserved_trends, an aggregate-pretrend partial-identification kernel under missing cohort prehistories, and pid_dynamic_iv_compliance_v2, a response-contribution memory-bounds kernel for two-stage one-sided IV designs. Both are partial-ID adjacent, but neither concerns continuous treatment, bounded hidden confounding without an instrument, or monotone-concave dose-response curve closure.","published_axis":"The closest published comparator added in v3 is BaitairianSebastienJreichKatsahianGuilloux2025, which gives sharp APO bounds under a continuous sensitivity model; the remaining close comparators are JessonDouglasManshausenSolalMeinshausenStierGalShalit2022 and MarmarelisHaddadJessonJahanshadGalstyanVerSteeg2023 for continuous-dose sensitivity bands, plus Manski1997 and OkumuraUsui2014 for shape-restricted treatment-response bounds. The epsilon-gap is that pointwise sharp APO or dose-response bounds do not by themselves prove a finite-grid joint pasting theorem through one latent treatment-density law after monotone-concave shape restrictions are imposed."}
seed_list: Prior work MarmarelisHaddadJessonJahanshadGalstyanVerSteeg2023 would imply that pointwise hidden-confounding dose bands summarize continuous-dose uncertainty. Our seed conjectures that a monotone-concave curve envelope is a strictly tighter and jointly sharp object, because concavity propagates endpoint guarantees across doses.

Prior work JessonDouglasManshausenSolalMeinshausenStierGalShalit2022 would predict that continuous marginal sensitivity bounds can be analyzed dose by dose. Our seed conjectures that neighboring doses cannot be optimized independently once diminishing returns is imposed, because one slope budget links the hidden-confounding extrema.

Prior work DalalTchetgenTchetgen2025 would imply that exposure-varying sensitivity functions sharpen pointwise dose-response bounds. Our seed conjectures that monotone-concave shape can dominate a pointwise sensitivity-function relaxation at selected target doses, because chord constraints transmit high-dose lower guarantees backward.

Prior work Manski1997 establishes concave-monotone response bounds without modeling selection. Our seed conjectures that adding a bounded hidden-confounding envelope creates a three-dose phase transition absent from Manski's unrestricted-selection envelope, because sensitivity endpoints provide finite anchors for concavity.

Prior work OkumuraUsui2014 establishes sharp concave-MTR plus MTS bounds. Our seed conjectures that replacing MTS by symmetric bounded hidden confounding changes the active endpoint pattern, because selection is no longer one-sided but concavity still forces cross-dose interpolation.

Prior work WestlingGilbertCarone2020 would treat monotonicity as an estimation constraint for a point-identified dose curve. Our seed conjectures that under hidden confounding, isotonic or concave smoothing of point estimates is not an identified set, because the sharp object is the coordinate projection of interval-constrained shape polytope.

Prior work BonviniKennedyVenturaWasserman2022 bounds parameters of marginal structural models under continuous-treatment sensitivity. Our seed conjectures that a saturated dose-grid MSM with monotone-concave restrictions has active-set sharp bounds not visible in the low-dimensional MSM parameter interval.

Prior work YadlowskyNamkoongBasuDuchiTian2022 establishes sharp bounded-confounding CATE/ATE intervals for binary treatment. Our seed conjectures that adjacent binary dose slices become jointly nonindependent under concavity, because a single dose curve must satisfy decreasing slopes across all slices.

Prior work DornGuo2023 shows sharp IPW sensitivity via quantile balancing for a target estimand. Our seed conjectures that running sharp quantile balancing separately at each dose can still be curve-level nonsharp, because separate dose optimizers may violate monotone-concave compatibility.

Prior work Tan2006 assumes a marginal sensitivity model for hidden confounding. Our seed conjectures that adding monotone-concave dose response yields a finite LP certificate for saturation/minimum-effective-dose claims, because the sensitivity model supplies boxes and shape supplies the coupling inequalities.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work MarmarelisHaddadJessonJahanshadGalstyanVerSteeg2023 would imply that pointwise hidden-confounding dose bands summarize continuous-dose uncertainty. Our seed conjectures that a monotone-concave curve envelope is a strictly tighter and jointly sharp object, because concavity propagates endpoint guarantees across doses.

anchor_paper.author: Marmarelis, Haddad, Jesson, Jahanshad, Galstyan, Ver Steeg
anchor_paper.claim: Develops partial identification of average and conditional continuous-dose response curves under hidden confounding.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Not blocked: not merely adding shape; the mechanism is cross-dose compatibility and strict propagation of endpoint sensitivity guarantees.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from MarmarelisHaddadJessonJahanshadGalstyanVerSteeg2023 because one can post-process their bands with a concave projection.
seed_details.rebuttal: Post-processing is an outer operation; the gap is whether the resulting shape-closed band is jointly sharp under one hidden-confounding law.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work JessonDouglasManshausenSolalMeinshausenStierGalShalit2022 would predict that continuous marginal sensitivity bounds can be analyzed dose by dose. Our seed conjectures that neighboring doses cannot be optimized independently once diminishing returns is imposed, because one slope budget links the hidden-confounding extrema.

anchor_paper.author: Jesson, Douglas, Manshausen, Solal, Meinshausen, Stier, Gal, Shalit
anchor_paper.claim: Introduces a continuous-treatment marginal sensitivity model and scalable bounds for continuous-valued interventions.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Not blocked: the extension changes the feasible geometry by adding slope-budget coupling absent from pointwise CMSM bounds.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from their CMSM because shape constraints are just an extra convex constraint.
seed_details.rebuttal: The finite LP is routine, but joint causal sharpness of the convex constraint is not established by the CMSM paper.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work DalalTchetgenTchetgen2025 would imply that exposure-varying sensitivity functions sharpen pointwise dose-response bounds. Our seed conjectures that monotone-concave shape can dominate a pointwise sensitivity-function relaxation at selected target doses, because chord constraints transmit high-dose lower guarantees backward.

anchor_paper.author: Dalal and Tchetgen Tchetgen
anchor_paper.claim: Extends Rosenbaum and marginal sensitivity frameworks to endogenous continuous exposures with dose-varying sensitivity functions and pseudo-outcome regression bounds.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Not blocked: the phenomenon is a target-dose bound reversal from chord propagation, not a generic continuous-treatment extension.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from DalalTchetgenTchetgen2025 because their exposure-varying functions already produce sharper bands.
seed_details.rebuttal: Their sharpness is pointwise for the sensitivity model; the proposed claim concerns curve-level shape closure and active-dose propagation.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work Manski1997 establishes concave-monotone response bounds without modeling selection. Our seed conjectures that adding a bounded hidden-confounding envelope creates a three-dose phase transition absent from Manski's unrestricted-selection envelope, because sensitivity endpoints provide finite anchors for concavity.

anchor_paper.author: Manski
anchor_paper.claim: Derives what can be learned about treatment response under monotone, semi-monotone, and concave-monotone response assumptions without selection assumptions.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Not blocked: this is not just weakening/strengthening Manski; bounded-confounding endpoints create a new finite-grid phase transition.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from Manski1997 because concave-MTR already gives the relevant bounds.
seed_details.rebuttal: Manski's unrestricted-selection envelope lacks the modern sensitivity endpoints whose chord interaction is the proposed phase-transition mechanism.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work OkumuraUsui2014 establishes sharp concave-MTR plus MTS bounds. Our seed conjectures that replacing MTS by symmetric bounded hidden confounding changes the active endpoint pattern, because selection is no longer one-sided but concavity still forces cross-dose interpolation.

anchor_paper.author: Okumura and Usui
anchor_paper.claim: Identifies sharp bounds on mean treatment response and ATE under concave-MTR and monotone treatment selection.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Not blocked: the change produces a different active endpoint pattern, not a generic assumption replacement.
seed_details.referee_objection: This follows by swapping their MTS inequalities for sensitivity inequalities.
seed_details.rebuttal: The swap changes the geometry from one-sided selection order to dose-wise hidden-confounding boxes, and sharp joint realization remains open.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work WestlingGilbertCarone2020 would treat monotonicity as an estimation constraint for a point-identified dose curve. Our seed conjectures that under hidden confounding, isotonic or concave smoothing of point estimates is not an identified set, because the sharp object is the coordinate projection of interval-constrained shape polytope.

anchor_paper.author: Westling, Gilbert, Carone
anchor_paper.claim: Develops nonparametric causal isotonic regression for a monotone point-identified dose-response curve.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Not blocked: this reveals an identification gap hidden by estimator-level shape constraints.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is just interval-valued isotonic regression.
seed_details.rebuttal: Interval-valued isotonic regression gives the convex object, but the causal novelty is whether hidden-confounding sensitivity realizes every interval-shaped curve.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work BonviniKennedyVenturaWasserman2022 bounds parameters of marginal structural models under continuous-treatment sensitivity. Our seed conjectures that a saturated dose-grid MSM with monotone-concave restrictions has active-set sharp bounds not visible in the low-dimensional MSM parameter interval.

anchor_paper.author: Bonvini, Kennedy, Ventura, Wasserman
anchor_paper.claim: Develops sensitivity analysis for marginal structural models with discrete, continuous, static, and time-varying treatments.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Not blocked: the active-set phenomenon appears when the target is the whole curve/grid, not a finite MSM coefficient alone.
seed_details.referee_objection: A saturated MSM basis already covers the dose grid.
seed_details.rebuttal: A saturated basis does not by itself impose monotone-concave shape or prove joint sharpness of shape-constrained coefficient intervals.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work YadlowskyNamkoongBasuDuchiTian2022 establishes sharp bounded-confounding CATE/ATE intervals for binary treatment. Our seed conjectures that adjacent binary dose slices become jointly nonindependent under concavity, because a single dose curve must satisfy decreasing slopes across all slices.

anchor_paper.author: Yadlowsky, Namkoong, Basu, Duchi, Tian
anchor_paper.claim: Gives sharp conditional and average treatment-effect bounds under bounded unobserved confounding for binary treatment.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Not blocked: the target reveals a cross-slice identification gap, not a CATE/ATE variant of the same binary result.
seed_details.referee_objection: Apply their binary result to every adjacent dose contrast.
seed_details.rebuttal: Separate adjacent contrasts need not paste into one nondecreasing concave level curve.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work DornGuo2023 shows sharp IPW sensitivity via quantile balancing for a target estimand. Our seed conjectures that running sharp quantile balancing separately at each dose can still be curve-level nonsharp, because separate dose optimizers may violate monotone-concave compatibility.

anchor_paper.author: Dorn and Guo
anchor_paper.claim: Derives sharp IPW sensitivity bounds under Tan's marginal sensitivity model and shows earlier bounds can be too wide.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Not blocked: the possible nonsharpness is a new cross-dose compatibility issue after each pointwise bound is already sharp.
seed_details.referee_objection: Quantile balancing is sharp, so there is nothing left to identify.
seed_details.rebuttal: Sharpness for one estimand does not imply simultaneous sharpness for a monotone-concave vector of estimands.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work Tan2006 assumes a marginal sensitivity model for hidden confounding. Our seed conjectures that adding monotone-concave dose response yields a finite LP certificate for saturation/minimum-effective-dose claims, because the sensitivity model supplies boxes and shape supplies the coupling inequalities.

anchor_paper.author: Tan
anchor_paper.claim: Develops a distributional/propensity-score sensitivity approach for causal inference under hidden confounding.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Not blocked: the seed is orthogonal to simply applying Tan; the new content is finite shape-coupled dose certification.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is just a linear program after Tan's sensitivity model.
seed_details.rebuttal: The LP outer bound is easy; the proposed publishable question is sharpness and minimal phase-transition structure for continuous-dose curves.

proposed_from.literature_map: Manski1990 (AER, Manski1990) gives bounded-outcome partial-ID logic for treatment effects; open tension is that it is not dose-curve or shape-specific. Manski1997 (Econometrica, Manski1997) gives monotone, semi-monotone, and concave-monotone treatment-response bounds without assumptions on selection; gap is bounded hidden confounding for continuous exposure. ManskiPepper2000 (Econometrica, ManskiPepper2000) combines monotone response/selection/instruments; tension is that the current topic explicitly has no instrument. OkumuraUsui2014 (Quantitative Economics, OkumuraUsui2014) gives sharp concave-MTR plus MTS bounds; gap is replacing MTS with a modern sensitivity model. Imbens2000 and HiranoImbens2004 establish generalized-propensity dose-response identification under observed confounding; gap is sensitivity to hidden confounding. KennedyMaMcHughSmall2017 (JRSSB) gives doubly robust continuous-treatment curve estimation under unconfoundedness; gap is partial ID. WestlingGilbertCarone2020 (JRSSB) gives causal isotonic regression for monotone point-identified curves; tension is estimation versus identified-set geometry. Tan2006 (JASA) gives the marginal sensitivity-model ancestor. YadlowskyNamkoongBasuDuchiTian2022 (Annals of Statistics) gives sharp binary-treatment CATE/ATE hidden-confounding bounds; gap is continuous-dose curve shape. DornGuo2023 (JASA) shows nonsharp sensitivity intervals can be asymptotically too wide; tension is whether pointwise continuous-dose bands remain jointly sharp under shape. BonviniKennedyVenturaWasserman2022 (arXiv) handles sensitivity for marginal structural models with continuous treatments; gap is shape-restricted curve projection. JessonDouglasManshausenSolalMeinshausenStierGalShalit2022 (NeurIPS) and MarmarelisHaddadJessonJahanshadGalstyanVerSteeg2023 (UAI) develop continuous-dose hidden-confounding bounds; open problem is sharp curve-level shape closure. DalalTchetgenTchetgen2025 (arXiv) extends Rosenbaum/MSM sensitivity to endogenous continuous exposures with dose-varying sensitivity; gap is monotone-concave sharpness. BalkePearl1997 is reused from pid_dynamic_iv_compliance_v2 as generic sharp-LP partial-ID context, but the current proposal is non-IV and shape/sensitivity-based.

banked_reason: Stage -0.5 ACCEPT angle 0 v3 tier=field; Stage 0.5 REJECT (novelty) — density-version obstruction collapses the only field-tier kernel; surviving result is generic Manski-style outer containment plus monotone-concave LP projection.
